title: Leaf Space partners with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test connectivity service
summary: Leaf Space has partnered with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test a new space connectivity service called TreeNet, which aims to improve seamless space communications. The service was unveiled on May 27 by the Italian ground segment operator.

title: Astronauts briefly shelter in Dragon during ISS leak repair
summary: NASA astronauts on the ISS sheltered in a Dragon spacecraft while cosmonauts repaired an air leak in a Russian module. The incident occurred on June 5.

title: Next Ariane 6 launch to carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites using upgraded boosters
summary: The next Ariane 6 launch will carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites, marking the largest number of broadband satellites flown to date by Amazon. This mission will also be the first to use upgraded boosters.

title: Let’s build the moon base, but not lose sight of Mars
summary: NASA and the space industry are experiencing significant advancements, highlighted by the Artemis 2 crew's lunar flyby. The focus remains on building a moon base while maintaining Mars exploration goals.

title: Qianfan constellation deployment hits 200 satellites with Long March 8 and 6A launches
summary: The Qianfan constellation, led by Shanghai, reached 200 satellites with two recent Long March 8 and 6A launches. This follows two experimental flights and accelerates the deployment of the constellation.

title: Satellite maker Apex’s valuation rises to $2.3 billion after latest $200 million raise
summary: Apex, a satellite manufacturer, raised $200 million in a funding round, increasing its valuation to $2.3 billion. The company plans to expand its in-house satellite production capabilities with the new funds.

title: The Exploration Company completes drop test of Nyx capsule
summary: The Exploration Company successfully conducted a drop test of its Nyx spacecraft, designed for cargo transport to low Earth orbit. This milestone precedes a planned 2028 test flight.

title: Axiom Space adds more than $175 million to funding round
summary: Axiom Space raised over $175 million in its latest funding round, with Japan’s largest bank joining as an investor.

title: HASC NDAA markup challenges Space Force on satellite programs
summary: The House Armed Services Committee (HASC) is challenging the U.S. Space Force by seeking to preserve a missile-warning satellite program that the Pentagon aims to cancel. It also criticizes recent satellite procurement and GPS oversight decisions.

title: AstroForge completes DeepSpace-2 spacecraft
summary: Asteroid mining startup AstroForge has finished building its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft for a planned launch later in 2024. The company incorporated lessons from a previous failed mission to improve the new spacecraft.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 213 — Live From ISDC With Gerry Griffin
summary: The 213th episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a live interview with Apollo legend Gerry Griffin at the International Space Development Conference (ISDC) in McLean, Virginia. The episode was hosted by Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik.

title: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates new trailer for upcoming Broadway musical, 'Galileo'
summary: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates a new trailer for the upcoming Broadway musical 'Galileo', featuring Raúl Esparza in the lead role of the Renaissance astronomer. The musical is set to premiere in the fall.

title: I resisted smart telescopes for years — then one changed my life
summary: The article discusses a personal experience with smart telescopes, highlighting their benefits for deep-sky stargazing during spring. It emphasizes the transformative impact of using such technology for astronomy.

title: Going supersonic! NASA's X-59 jet breaks sound barrier for the 1st time
summary: NASA's X-59 jet achieved supersonic flight for the first time on June 5, reaching a speed of Mach 1.1. This milestone marks a significant step in quiet supersonic technology development.

title: DC Studios lights the fuse on a sensational final 'Supergirl' trailer and it's totally peak (video)
summary: DC Studios released the final trailer for the upcoming 'Supergirl' film, highlighting differing perspectives on heroism between the protagonist and her cousin. The trailer is generating significant buzz ahead of the movie's release.

title: Inside the cruel confines of Apple TV's 'Star City' with star Rhys Ifans and series creators (interview)
summary: Apple TV's 'Star City' explores danger on the ground rather than in space, as highlighted by star Rhys Ifans and series creators. The interview contrasts the show's terrestrial risks with the space-based dangers of 'For All Mankind'.

title: Scientists find wind blowing from our Milky Way's black hole after half-century search: 'There it is'
summary: Astronomers have detected powerful winds emanating from Sagittarius A*, the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way, after a 50-year search. The discovery confirms long-held theories about the black hole's influence on its surroundings.

title: Astrophotographer captures colossal 'Godzilla' plasma cloud stalking the edge of the sun (video)
summary: An astrophotographer captured a massive solar prominence resembling a 'Godzilla' plasma cloud on the sun's edge, showcasing coronal rain flowing along magnetic field lines.

title: Astronauts on International Space Station take shelter in SpaceX Dragon as cosmonauts try to fix air leak
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station took shelter in a SpaceX Dragon capsule due to an air leak on the Russian side of the station. Cosmonauts worked to fix the persistent leak.

title: NASA’s X-59 Aircraft Flies Supersonic for First Time
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft achieved supersonic flight for the first time, reaching Mach 1.1 during an 81-minute test flight. The milestone paves the way for demonstrating its quiet supersonic capabilities later in 2024.

title: NASA Announces Winners of 2026 University Innovation Competition
summary: NASA announced MIT's project on lunar power systems as the winner of the 2026 RASC-AL competition, with other teams from MIT and Virginia Tech also placing highly.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission Research Continues on Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II Moon Mission research is ongoing on Earth after the crew safely returned from their lunar flyby mission. Scientists are analyzing data to support future deep space human exploration.

title: NASA Concludes Antenna Mishap Investigation, Releases Report
summary: NASA has concluded its investigation into the damage to the Deep Space Station 14 (DSS-14) antenna at the Goldstone Deep Space Communications Complex, classifying it as a Type A mishap. The agency has released a report on the incident.

title: First Steps: America’s Grueling Second Spacewalk
summary: NASA planned a challenging second spacewalk one year after its first, assigned to Gene Cernan on Gemini IX-A after Gemini VIII's early mission end. The EVA took place on June 5, 1966.

title: Fighting Fire With Fire
summary: Prescribed burns are used in Australia's Northern Territory to reduce the severity of wildfires during the fire season. This practice helps manage fire-prone ecosystems.

title: NASA Hosts 2026 Review on Advanced Composite Manufacturing
summary: NASA's Hi-Rate Composite Aircraft Manufacturing (HiCAM) project held a 2026 spring review meeting at Langley Research Center, bringing together 150 participants from a 22-member public-private consortium to assess progress.

title: NASA-Funded Study Shows Wildfire Smoke’s Hidden Ozone Toll
summary: A NASA-funded study reveals that wildfire smoke has significantly increased ground-level ozone pollution across much of the contiguous U.S. over the past decade, posing health risks even in areas far from active fires.

title: Colorful, Chaotic Jupiter
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured a color-enhanced image of Jupiter's chaotic northern hemisphere during its 61st close flyby on May 12, 2024. Citizen scientist Gary Eason processed the raw JunoCam data to improve color and clarity.

title: A Moonlit Earth as Seen From Artemis II
summary: An astronaut aboard Artemis II captured a striking photo of Earth from space, highlighting its beauty and fragility. This image offers a new perspective on our planet's place in the cosmos.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn rocket anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is shifting focus to upcoming missions while addressing the setback.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is assessing damage from an anomaly and identifying future danger areas. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for major operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for NASA's Artemis III mission have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparations for the upcoming lunar mission despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and lunar landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions planned from five different entities.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. The discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36 (LC-36). The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, focusing on rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative builds on existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for follow-up missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing materials to ensure they survive the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is supported by these innovative minds.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and innovation in the space sector.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news highlights the importance of sensitivity and sturdiness in space industry components. No specific company or detailed context is provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry news article titled 'The great parachute bake-out' suggests a focus on parachute testing or technology for space missions. This could involve high-temperature testing of parachutes for re-entry or descent systems.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to prevent microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate rendezvous capabilities with an asteroid. This mission will pave the way for future asteroid mining efforts by the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This is the third time in about 14 months that Apex has secured a $200 million round.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The U.S. is expanding its commercial space station initiatives to include Europe, ensuring continued access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) as the International Space Station (ISS) nears retirement. This move highlights growing international collaboration in the commercial space sector.

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap highlights differentiation in revenue recognition compared to hardware-focused companies. CFO and President Ashley Johnson emphasizes their unique approach to financial outcomes.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company completed a Nyx drop test on May 19 to evaluate parachute performance and vehicle dynamics for a controlled Earth landing. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to assess the capsule's landing capabilities.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times the size of its predecessor, Condor-XL. The new satellite bus is designed to cater to the emerging orbital data center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to revert to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement strategy. The move aims to align with industry feedback and reshape the path forward for lunar missions.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to return to flight operations by the end of this year, following assessments of launch pad and infrastructure damage. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the timeline in a post on X.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating the Space Development Agency (SDA) and Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO) within the Pentagon, which are tasked with accelerating space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the Pentagon's efforts to modernize and streamline space programs.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a 50% deployment waiver for its Project Kuiper constellation by the end of July, but imposed conditions on spectrum priority.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Apex Space, a satellite bus manufacturer, raised an additional $200 million in capital, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, beginning with the PExT demonstration mission. The Polylingual Experimental Terminal (PExT) mission has been launched as part of this initiative.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services for maritime applications. This collaboration aims to enhance connectivity solutions in the maritime sector.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million option for maritime domain awareness using AI-enabled data analytics. This extends a contract initiated last year to support U.S. Combatant Commands.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites, 'Tom & Jerry', for a LEO rendezvous and inspection mission. The mission aims to demonstrate maneuvering and inspection capabilities in Low-Earth Orbit.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics and NTT have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on developing a high-speed optical communications infrastructure for space. The partnership aims to enhance space communications through advanced photonics technology.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform named Condor-Ultra, aimed at supporting orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for 2025.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite propulsion systems.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the NYSE, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company is an aerospace and defense firm.
